title: Fixing the Node.js Express Middleware Dynamic Route Resolution

statement.markdown: You are given a Node.js Express application with a dynamic route resolver middleware. However, the middleware is not correctly resolving routes for URLs with query parameters. Your task is to fix the bug in the middleware to make it work correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a URL string in the format `/users?name=John&age=30`.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the resolved route path, e.g. `/users`.
statement.notes: The middleware should ignore query parameters and only consider the route path.

examples.explanation: Ignore query parameters and return the route path
Ignore query parameters and return the route path

editorial.title: Editorial: Fixing the Dynamic Route Resolution Middleware
editorial.intuition: The middleware should ignore query parameters and only consider the route path.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Split the URL by '?' and take the first part
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the URL class to parse the URL and get the pathname
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The output should be the resolved route path, ignoring query parameters
editorial.dryRun: Example usage: `const url = new URL('/users?name=John&age=30'); console.log(url.pathname);`
